title: Australia’s insurance rates continue downward trend
full_text:
Cookies, device or similar online identifiers (e.g. login-based identifiers, randomly assigned identifiers, network based identifiers) together with other information (e.g. browser type and information, language, screen size, supported technologies etc.) can be stored or read on your device to recognise it each time it connects to an app or to a website, for one or several of the purposes presented here.
Illustrations
- Most purposes explained in this notice rely on the storage or accessing of information from your device when you use an app or visit a website. For example, a vendor or publisher might need to store a cookie on your device during your first visit on a website, to be able to recognise your device during your next visits (by accessing this cookie each time).
Number of Vendors seeking consent: 818
summary: Australia's insurance rates are experiencing a continued downward trend, reflecting broader economic conditions.
publication:
Chalmers is going for broke. Will we end up a poorer country?
The Australian09 May 20263,403 wordsMATTHEW CRANSTON

BUDGET 2026 If Australians believe Jim Chalmers, then this Tuesday’s budget is meant to be the most consequential in decades. In front of more than one million viewers the Treasurer will frame his speech by saying the budget will not be one ...
heading: General Insurance
page_title: Australia’s insurance rates continue downward trend - Business Insurance
key_points: Insurance rates in Australia are decreasing.
The trend is linked to economic factors and market competition.
Potential implications for risk assessment and pricing strategies.
relevance: Understanding the trends in insurance rates is crucial for actuaries and financial services professionals in pricing and risk management.
